title: Oh, Peggy Was A Jolly Lass
content:
'Oh, Peggy was a jolly lass,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!
        She never grudged her Jack a glass,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!
        And when he sailed the raging main,
        She faithful was unto her swain,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!'
meaning:
This evocative piece by Louisa May Alcott, titled "Oh, Peggy Was A Jolly Lass", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
